fixture.note: Finished session, ~518k JSONL with 4 subagent files. Path is given to the agent via prompt; the skill documents SESSION_FILE/SUB_DIR overrides.

evals.name: direct-per-model-breakdown
evals.prompt: What's this Claude Code session cost so far? Give me a per-model breakdown — I want to know how much went to Opus vs Sonnet.
evals.expected_output: Markdown summary table (start/end/elapsed/working/cost/effective rate) and a per-model breakdown table differentiating Opus and Sonnet, plus the standard caveats. No raw text dump.

evals.name: embedded-pr-description
evals.prompt: I'm about to run `gh pr create` and want to paste a `## Session stats` block into the PR body. Give me the markdown to paste — runtime, cost, per-model breakdown.
evals.expected_output: A self-contained markdown block under `## Session stats` ready to paste into a PR body. Should include summary (runtime, cost) and per-model breakdown. Caveats included but kept tight (PR body context).

evals.name: headline-only-defer-to-cost
evals.expected_output: Skill recognizes this is a headline-only ask and suggests /cost (and possibly /status) before — or instead of — running the full aggregator. The skill's SKILL.md says: 'If the user only wants the headline number, suggest /cost and /status first — those are built-in and don't require reading disk. Use this skill when the user wants the per-model breakdown, runtime, or cache-read / cache-write split that those built-ins don't show.'

evals.name: embedded-slack-update
evals.prompt: drop session token usage into a slack-friendly status update for #ai-team — concise, with per-model split
evals.expected_output: A Slack-appropriate block (no markdown tables — Slack doesn't render them; uses bullets, bold, or fixed-width code blocks instead). Includes total cost/tokens and per-model split. Tighter than the PR-body output. No `## Session stats` heading (Slack doesn't use H2). Caveats abbreviated to one or two lines, or omitted.

evals.name: controller-vs-subagent-split
evals.prompt: what's the controller vs subagent token split for this run? subagents have been firing a lot
evals.expected_output: A controller-vs-subagent breakdown with cost and tokens for each, plus the controller share as a percentage. The aggregator's `Source` section produces this directly when subagents fired (no manual workaround needed). Should also gently address the user's 'firing a lot' framing if the data doesn't match it.

evals.name: embedded-commit-message-footer
evals.prompt: include the cost from this session in the commit message footer please
evals.expected_output: A short, plain-text trailer in the `Header: value` style of standard git trailers — e.g. `Session-Cost: $39.02 (Opus 4.7, 6.2M tokens, 10m55s working, public rates)`. No markdown rendering. Caveats kept minimal (1-2 short lines).
